name: jsonschema-to-openapi

metadata.name: jsonschema-to-openapi
metadata.summary: Converts a JSON schema to an OpenAPI specification version 3.0.

documentation:
# jsonschema-to-openapi

Converts a JSON schema to an OpenAPI specification version 3.0.

## Installation

```
pip install jsonschema-to-openapi
```

## Usage

#### CLI

```shell
jsonschema-to-openapi my_json_schema.json my_open_api_spec.json
```

### Package

```python
>>> from jsonschema_to_openapi.convert import convert

>>> schema = {} # Your json schema as dictionary.

>>> convert(schema)
{} # Your OpenAPI specification as dictionary.
```

## Caveats and Limitations

* If you have a [complex schema](https://json-schema.org/understanding-json-schema/structuring.html), where you extend a base schema with the `$ref` operator, you will need to put all base schemas under a "definitions" key in root.
* Abitrarily nested `xOf`s cannot be resolved yet. The program won't fail and will procude valid OpenAPI specification, but the result will still contain nested `xOf` operators. 
    * Input:

        ```json
        {
          "anyOf": [{"$ref": "#/definitions/my_object"}, { "anyOf": [{"type": "null"}] }],
          "definitions": {
              "my_object": {"type": "string"}
          }
        }
        ```
    * Output:

        ```json
        {
            "anyOf": [
                {
                    "$ref": "#/definitions/my_object"
                },
                {
                    "anyOf": [
                        {
                            "type": "null"
                        }
                    ]
                }
            ],
            "definitions": {
                "my_object": {
                    "nullable": false,
                    "type": "string"
                }
            }
        }
        ```

## Changelog

Please take a look at the [CHANGELOG.md](CHANGELOG.md) for notable changes to jsonschema-to-openapi.

## License

See the [LICENSE](LICENSE) for details.

## Development

We welcome new contributions to this project!

### Source Code

You can check the latest source code with the command:

```
git clone git@gitlab.com:InstaffoOpenSource/DataScience/jsonschema-to-openapi.git
```

### Linting

After cloning and installing the dependencies, you can lint the project by executing:

```
make lint
```

### Testing

After cloning and installing the dependencies, you can test the project by executing:

```
make test
```

## Help and Support

### Authors

- Jan-Benedikt Jagusch <jan@instaffo.de>

## Acknowledgements

- This project started as a Python port of [json-schema-to-openapi-schema](https://github.com/wework/json-schema-to-openapi-schema).

modules.name: jsonschema_to_openapi.exceptions
documentation: This module contains exceptions for this package.

classes.name: jsonschema_to_openapi.exceptions.JsonschemaToOpenapiException
documentation: Base exception for this package.

classes.name: jsonschema_to_openapi.exceptions.SchemaError
documentation: Error when there is something wrong with the schema.

classes.name: jsonschema_to_openapi.exceptions.InvalidTypeError
documentation: Exception when the type is invalid.

classes.name: jsonschema_to_openapi.exceptions.TooManyTypesError
documentation: Error when there are too many types.

modules.name: jsonschema_to_openapi
documentation: This package provides a function to convert JSON schema to an OpenAPI specification.

modules.name: jsonschema_to_openapi.cli
documentation: This module provides a cli tool for the package.

functions.name: jsonschema_to_openapi.cli.main
documentation: The main function for the cli.

modules.name: jsonschema_to_openapi.__main__
documentation: Allow package to be executable through `python -m jsonschema_to_openapi`.

modules.name: jsonschema_to_openapi.utils
documentation: This package provides utility functions.

modules.name: jsonschema_to_openapi.utils.logger
documentation: Provides a utils function to setup the logger.

functions.name: jsonschema_to_openapi.utils.logger.setup_logger
documentation:
Get the logger. The log level is set through the `LOG_LEVEL` environment variable.

Args:
    logger_name (str): The name of the logger.
Returns:
    logging.Logger: The logger.

modules.name: jsonschema_to_openapi.utils.io
documentation: This module contains functions for reading and writing.

functions.name: jsonschema_to_openapi.utils.io.read_json
documentation:
Reads a jsonfile from a filepath.

Args:
    filepath (str): The filepath.

Returns:
    dict: The json.

functions.name: jsonschema_to_openapi.utils.io.write_json
documentation:
Writes an object as .json into a filepath.

Args:
    obj (dict): The object.
    filepath (str): The filepath.

modules.name: jsonschema_to_openapi.convert
documentation: Converts a JSON schema to an OpenAPI specification 3.0.

functions.name: jsonschema_to_openapi.convert._validate_type
documentation:
Validates the "type" property.
The type can be a string or an array of strings.

functions.name: jsonschema_to_openapi.convert._strip_illegal_keywords
documentation: Strips illegal keywords from the schema.

functions.name: jsonschema_to_openapi.convert._convert_types
documentation: Converts the "type" property. Turns `type="null"` into `nullable=True`.

functions.name: jsonschema_to_openapi.convert._rewrite_constants
documentation: Rewrites a "constant" into an "enum" with only one value.

functions.name: jsonschema_to_openapi.convert._convert_dependencies
documentation:
Converts the dependencies property by appending a condition to the "allOf"
property.

functions.name: jsonschema_to_openapi.convert._rewrite_if_then_else
documentation:
Rewrites the "if", "then" and "else" properties by converting it into a "oneOf"
property.

functions.name: jsonschema_to_openapi.convert._rewrite_exclusive_min_max
documentation:
Rewrites the "exclusiveMaximum" and "exclusiveMinimum" properties by setting the
"maximum" and "minimum" values and setting the "exclusiveX" value to `True`.

functions.name: jsonschema_to_openapi.convert._convert_properties
documentation:
Converts the "patternProperty" into the "x-patternProperty".
Always explicitly sets the "additionalProperties" property.
Only applies to schemas with `type="object"`.

functions.name: jsonschema_to_openapi.convert._convert_any_of_null
documentation:
If the "anyOf" property contains `{"type": "null"}`, set `nullable=True` and
remove that condition from the "anyOf".

functions.name: jsonschema_to_openapi.convert._avoid_x_ofs
documentation:
If an "anyOf", "oneOf" or "allOf" property only contains one condition, set that
condition on the root of the schema and remove the "XOf" property.

functions.name: jsonschema_to_openapi.convert._convert
documentation: Converts a (sub) schema.

functions.name: jsonschema_to_openapi.convert.convert
documentation:
Converts a JSON schema to an OpenAPI specification 3.0.

Args:
    schema (dict): The JSON schema.

Returns:
    dict: The OpenAPIv3 specification.
